claim: Persona is framed as 'any MCP-aware agent' but the real user is n=1 (Shon, Claude Code primary); cross-agent claims are unvalidated portability, not a user-validated need.
The knowledge-vs-memory boundary is asserted as definitional and lint-enforced, but agent-derived synthesis is a real Shon workflow; risk of forced parallel substrate within 90 days of use.
evidence: Resolved at PM-Review 2026-04-26T02:05:00-07:00: memory systems address the 'personality problem' (preferences, observations, agent-side state); llm-corpus addresses the 'experience problem' (the user's domain knowledge that an LLM trained on broad data does not have). Different objects with different lifecycles; the boundary IS validated under that framing. Captured in AG-003 rationale.
mitigation: Personality-vs-experience framing recorded in AG-003 rationale. Stage 2 anti-goal scope language must preserve this distinction; agent-derived synthesis lives in memory frameworks, not the corpus.

claim: Stated success metric (50-query harness, hit-rate >=0.85) depends on a labeled eval set that does not exist and is listed as Future Work in the whitepaper.
evidence: Replaced at Frame stage with M-001 (weekly grounded-query rate + acceptance) which is measurable from v1 ship; formal harness deferred to v1.5 per AG-005.
mitigation: M-001 north-star + AG-005 deferral; instrument (MCP telemetry + corpus mark-accepted CLI) ships in v1.

claim: Maya R. (P-001) objects to A-006: evidence_required only verifies that both Claude Code and Gemini CLI can call corpus.find and return ranked results, not that ranking quality is equivalent across agents — assumption can pass while cross-agent UX still degrades.
evidence: Accepted at PM-Review 2026-04-26T02:05:00-07:00: Shon trusts the team to resolve adequately at Stage 2.
mitigation: Stage 2 to expand A-006 evidence_required to include retrieval-quality parity test (e.g., top-3 Jaccard overlap on a shared 10-query script).

claim: David Okafor (P-002) objects to A-007: lint-rule-only enforcement of local boundary is insufficient for high-severity privileged-data use case — transitive deps, model telemetry, and future contributors can introduce egress that import-grep won't catch.
evidence: Accepted at PM-Review 2026-04-26T02:05:00-07:00: Shon trusts the team to resolve adequately at Stage 2.
mitigation: Stage 2 to promote runtime egress audit from optional to required for SOL-005-A; consider process-level network namespace deny or eBPF egress monitor.

claim: Priya (P-003) objects to A-008: the 'within 30% of Claude Code baseline' tool-use-rate tolerance is acceptable as a portability claim but inadequate for the persona whose primary reasoning engine is local — for OPP-006 the local-LLM tool-use rate is the entire product, not a degraded fallback.
evidence: Confirmed at PM-Review 2026-04-26T02:05:00-07:00: cloud-baseline framing is unreliable; relative threshold rejected.
mitigation: Stage 2 MUST redefine A-008 success threshold as an absolute target (e.g., >=N corpus.find calls per N llama queries). The relative-to-cloud-baseline framing is invalid and must not appear in Spec.

claim: Council recommends CONDITIONAL_GO contingent on (1) persona audit confirming P-002/P-003 differentiation per AG-004, (2) Stage 2 SUCCESS-METRICS Spec edition adding measurable per-opportunity metrics for OPP-003, OPP-005, OPP-008.
evidence: Accepted at PM-Review 2026-04-26T02:05:00-07:00 as part of CONDITIONAL_GO approval.
mitigation: Both conditions covered by existing carry-forward to Stage 2; tracked as gate conditions in handoffs/stage-1-to-2.md.

claim: Carry-forward amnesia: Stage 2 workflow Step 1 only validates handoff existence; never quotes next_stage_focus to Architect. CF-1..CF-5 from stage-1-to-2 handoff would be silently dropped without orchestrator-supplied payload.
evidence: RedTeam pre-flight 2026-04-27T01:36 — orchestrator analyzed Stages/2-spec/workflow.md Step 1 and confirmed no next_stage_focus ingestion.
mitigation: Orchestrator passes 5 carry-forward items verbatim in skill invocation payload; gate post-check verifies CF-1..CF-5 landed in artifacts.

claim: AG-004 silent violation: Architect default prompt only reads OPPORTUNITY-TREE.yaml; would let cross-agent OPP-006 candidates be promoted to priority:must without anti-goal cross-check.
evidence: RedTeam pre-flight 2026-04-27T01:36 — orchestrator analyzed Stages/2-spec/workflow.md Step 4 Architect prompt and confirmed only OPP-TREE in input list.
mitigation: Architect prompt overridden to also read ANTI-GOALS.md; OPP-006/Gemini/Codex/Ollama candidates auto-tagged quality_flag:conflicts-anti-goal and capped at priority:should; gate post-check greps must-haves.

claim: SUCCESS-METRICS.md drift: workflow has no step that writes to it; CF-4 (per-opportunity hard metrics for OPP-003/005/008) would be silently skipped.
evidence: RedTeam pre-flight 2026-04-27T01:36 — orchestrator analyzed Stages/2-spec/workflow.md Steps 1-15 and confirmed no SUCCESS-METRICS write step.
mitigation: Orchestrator added explicit step to extend SUCCESS-METRICS.md with M-002 (OPP-003), M-003 (OPP-005), M-004 (OPP-008) — all 6 fields each per gate M-09; gate post-check verifies presence.

claim: FR-003 hybrid ranking — small-implementation passing test possible if any of 4 signals contributes effectively zero weight in fusion (Goodhart F-1).
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27
mitigation: Added @adversary @f-1 scenario asserting Kendall tau between full and per-signal ablated rankings is below 0.95 on 100-query benchmark.

claim: FR-012 grammar-constrained classification — implementation could pass scenarios via post-hoc retry rather than token-level grammar (Goodhart F-6).
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27
mitigation: Added @adversary @f-6 scenario asserting first-attempt schema-validity with retry_count=0 against grammar-eliciting stress prompt.

claim: NFR-002 egress hook missing UDP/DNS/HTTP2/TLS coverage — adversarial classifier could exfiltrate via DNS-over-UDP undetected (Goodhart F-12).
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27 + Privacy/Security Reviewer Council
mitigation: Added @adversary @f-12 Scenario Outline covering net.Socket, undici, dgram, dns.lookup, http2.connect, tls.connect — all blocked at in-process hook with egress.blocked telemetry.

claim: NFR-003 latency p95 attackable via result-cache memoization on benchmark queries (Goodhart F-13).
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27
mitigation: Added @adversary @f-13 scenario requiring 100 unseen queries from 10000-query distribution + instrumented assertion that no result-memoization layer fired.

claim: NFR-006 metric undefined when failure-lane denominator = 0; trivially passable by tuning validation to never fail (Goodhart F-15).
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27
mitigation: Added @adversary @f-15 scenario requiring N>=10 failure events before metric meaningful; below threshold reports inconclusive, not pass.

claim: NFR-002 sentinel-document scope insufficient for David persona (P-002) whose ENTIRE corpus is privileged — needs always-on guard, not sampled spot-check.
evidence: Persona Adversary 2026-04-27 (David objection)
mitigation: Added @david-objection scenario asserting in-process egress hook is active during ALL operations on ALL documents, with telemetry checkpoint per stage transition for entire 50-doc mixed-workload run.

claim: QATester F-2: error envelope error_code stable enumeration is referenced but not enumerated in scenarios — impl returning single 'error' code passes.
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27 (medium severity)
mitigation: Carry-forward: add scenario in Stage 5 pre-build enumerating required error_code values (query_required, invalid_filter, ranking_signal_unavailable, document_not_found, index_locked, server_initializing).

claim: QATester F-5: FR-010 MIME-sniff not exercised — only extension-allowlist tested; binary file with .md extension would pass.
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27 (high severity, deferred)
mitigation: Carry-forward: add scenario in Validate stage where file with allowed extension but binary MIME signature is rejected with reason 'mime_mismatch'.

claim: QATester F-8: FR-016a idempotency tests serial re-runs only — concurrent invocations of same stage on same doc not exercised.
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27 (high severity, deferred)
mitigation: Carry-forward: add scenario in Validate stage where same stage invoked concurrently for same document — assert exactly one row exists post-completion (UPSERT or unique-constraint enforced).

claim: QATester F-10: FR-017 content-hash idempotency tests entire-file hash only — first-4KB-only impl would falsely dedup files differing in tail.
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27 (high severity, deferred)
mitigation: Carry-forward: add scenario in Validate stage with two 60MB files identical in first 1MB but differing in tail — assert both ingest as separate documents.

claim: QATester F-3, F-4, F-7, F-9, F-11, F-14: 6 medium-severity Goodhart gaps — manifest auto-load delivery (F-3), recent-ingests timestamp source (F-4), vocabulary case/whitespace/unicode (F-7), checkpoint-write fsync interaction (F-9), failure-lane consistency under concurrent write (F-11), SIGKILL per-trial completeness ambiguity (F-14).
evidence: QATester adversary 2026-04-27 (6 medium severities batched)
mitigation: Carry-forward: address in Validate or Build/Test stage scenario refinements — each is non-blocking individually but worth tracking.

claim: NFR-008 absolute target value is null pending Validate spike data (Q-001); Plan stage cannot finalize threshold without Validate.
evidence: Architect candidate emission + Persona Adversary (Priya soft objection)
mitigation: Validate stage spike on Ollama+MCP integration MUST produce the absolute floor before Plan stage finalizes NFR-008.

claim: Council Stage 2 recommends conditional_go contingent on (1) all should-haves get ACs (now mitigated), (2) high-severity QATester findings addressed (F-1, F-6, F-12, F-13, F-15 mitigated; F-5, F-8, F-10 carry-forward), (3) NFR-002 always-on framing added (mitigated via C-016 scenario).
evidence: Council pass 2026-04-27
mitigation: Conditions 1 + 3 fully addressed pre-handoff; Condition 2 partially addressed (5 high-severity fixed inline; 3 high-severity F-5/F-8/F-10 carry-forward as C-018/019/020); user approved fix-before-handoff path at PM-Review.

claim: NFR-008 absolute floor N=20 is provisional (Plan stage); FINAL value depends on Stage 5 pilot run with FR-009 prompt template.
evidence: SP-006 + Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard reference data
mitigation: Stage 5 pilot run produces final N. If <20, escalate to PM-Review for NFR-008 either downgrade or product-side intervention (better prompt template).

claim: Cross-version Ollama model behavior — grammar-constrained generation tested on Qwen 2.5 7B; switching to different model family (Llama 3.x) may surface format-parameter quirks.
evidence: FEASIBILITY-STUDY Critical Risk #1
mitigation: NFR-004 100% benchmark per shipped model version; CI runs benchmark on each supported model before release.

claim: sqlite-vec exhaustive KNN degrades visibly past ~25k 384-dim vectors; v1 hard ceiling at 50k docs is conservative but corpus growth must be monitored.
evidence: Stage 2 Research + sqlite-vec issue #186
mitigation: NFR-003 explicit hard ceiling at 50k; warning at 25k via corpus.health resource (FR-021); sqlite-vec ANN index on roadmap monitoring.

claim: Prior Stage 3 Council ran on the same model (claude-opus-4-7) as the generator agents (Engineer/Research), violating gate.yaml generator_evaluator_check. Per recipe.yaml the evaluator MUST be claude-sonnet-4-6 when generator is claude-opus-4-7 (Panickssery/Bowman/He 2024 self-preference bias).
evidence: User report; gate.yaml line 122-128 generator_evaluator_check.on_violation=fatal; recipe.yaml line 66 model: claude-sonnet-4-6 pin
mitigation: Re-invoke Council on claude-sonnet-4-6 via Agent(model=sonnet); rebuild PM-Review summary with new Council recommendation; supersede prior pm-review-history entry for stage_from=3-validate. Root cause logged: imagined time pressure caused prior agent to bypass model separation.

claim: Desirability quadrant lacks a Stage-5-gateable early signal for A-002 (sustained ≥5 queries/wk). 2 of 3 desirability assumptions deferred to 6-operate; A-001 is reference-implementation only. Without an early-signal proxy, product can ship and instrument correctly yet still fail A-002 at Week 4 with no prior kill signal.
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Council perspective: Maya (curator persona) — citing A-001/A-002/A-009, UR-001, OPP-001, M-001
mitigation: Stage 4 Plan must add Week-1 engagement-proxy gate to Stage 5 build-test pilot: ≥5 corpus.find queries in first 7 days of dogfooding + ≥1 acceptance event before Stage 6 gate opens. Converts 30-day post-ship A-002 black box into a Stage-5-gated kill signal.

claim: NFR-002 runtime egress hook (D-013) covers 6 JS-land primitives (net.Socket, undici, dgram, dns, http2, tls) but NOT (a) Worker threads with their own module registry, (b) child_process.spawn launching curl/wget, (c) native Node addons making raw POSIX socket calls below the JS layer. SP-002 was paper-only; no tcpdump verification ran. D-013 reversibility=medium understates the architectural commitment.
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Council perspective: David (security persona) — citing NFR-001/002, A-007, D-013, SP-002, C-013, C-016
mitigation: Stage 4 Plan must (1) add tcpdump-verified integration test as Stage 5 build-ready gate condition, including synthetic Worker thread + child_process egress attempts, (2) document Worker/child_process/native-addon coverage gaps explicitly in D-013 amendment or new ADR, (3) revise D-013 reversibility from medium to low, (4) ensure hook also fires on import-time side-effect egress (not just runtime call-time).

claim: NFR-008 absolute floor N=20 (D-012) is provisional from cross-benchmark literature, not from FR-009 prompt template + live Ollama+MCP integration. If real rate falls below 20 on 7B-class models, Priya-persona value collapses with no pre-built remediation. Current carry-forward language specifies no model coverage requirement and no prompt-template iteration budget.
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Council perspective: Priya (local-LLM persona) — citing NFR-008, A-008, D-006/012, C-022, Q-001, SP-006, AG-004
mitigation: Stage 4 Plan ROADMAP must include a named NFR-008 pre-build pilot sprint BEFORE main build sprint with: (a) FR-009 prompt template authored and version-controlled, (b) 100-query benchmark on BOTH Llama 3.1 8B Instruct and Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct via live Ollama+MCP, (c) ≥2 prompt-variant iteration cycles if first run <N=25, (d) hard gate: if no variant achieves ≥20 on either model after 2 iterations, NFR-008 downgrades to nice_to_have BEFORE sprint 1 build, not after.

claim: Spike evidence quality across SP-001/003/004/005 is reference+literature analysis, not benchmark output. Workflow.md line 232 standard ('test_evidence MUST be concrete — a number, a quote from a real interview transcript, a benchmark output') is not met. SP-002 cites NodeShield arXiv but no live test ran. Only acknowledged inline; downstream gate criteria treat all 5 'passed' spikes as equivalent.
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Council perspective: methodology auditor — citing workflow.md line 232 anti-pattern, gate.yaml M-001 success_rate field, SP-001..SP-005 method narratives, prior PM-Review timing pattern (~3 min from D-008 batch to PM-Review approve)
mitigation: Stage 5 build-test plan MUST include real spike re-runs as build-ready conditions: actual 100-doc Ollama benchmark for SP-001/A-004, tcpdump test for SP-002 (subsumed by C-029), file-type benchmark on 50-file mixed corpus for SP-003, SQLite UPSERT race harness on parallel writers for SP-004, BERTopic N-parameter sweep on synthetic noisy corpus for SP-005. Status of these assumptions remains 'valid (carry-forward)' with confidence:medium until real evidence lands.

claim: All 8 ADRs (ADR-001..ADR-008) are status: proposed at Stage 4 PM-Review surface. PM-Review Step 7 must accept critical-path ADRs (ADR-001/002/003/004/007/008 are critical-path; ADR-005/006 back next-horizon items but ADR-005 gates SP-000 entry) before Stage 5 entry. Sprint 1 SP-001 entry criterion all_referenced_adrs_status_eq_accepted: [ADR-001] is unbuildable until ADR-001 status flips.
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Council Architect + DevEx perspectives — both flagged as blocking; ROADMAP.yaml line 19 all_critical_path_adrs_accepted: false
mitigation: PM-Review (Shon) at Stage 4 Step 7 accepts ADR-001..ADR-008 (or any subset; non-accepted ADRs gate their corresponding sprint entry only). Status flip to accepted with date_accepted populated.

claim: SP-003 exit criteria reference c_018_mime_mismatch_adversary_scenario_passes and c_020_full_file_hash_adversary_scenario_passes; SP-006 exit references c_019_concurrent_stage_adversary_scenario_passes. None of these adversary scenarios exist in ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature — they were Stage 2 'carry-forward to Validate' items that were deferred again. Build team will hit a wall on SP-003 day 1.
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Council QA perspective — explicit grep of ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature confirms F-5/F-8/F-10 / mime_mismatch / concurrent-invocation / 60MB-tail patterns absent
mitigation: Stage 5 SP-003 entry condition: add 3 Gherkin scenarios (@adversary @f-5, @adversary @f-8, @adversary @f-10) to ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature BEFORE SP-003 entry — written at SP-001 close or earlier. Update frontmatter scenarios_total from 99 to actual (128+3=131).

claim: SP-001 exit criterion tcpdump_test_on_non_loopback_during_pipeline_shows_zero_packets requires sudo / CAP_NET_RAW. ProductBuild has no specified mechanism for obtaining this capability. ADR-001 mentions tcpdump at build-ready gate but no privilege-acquisition recipe exists in the bundle.
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Council DevEx perspective; ADR-001 Compliance section cites tcpdump test but no sudo recipe; SP-001 exit will dead-end at 'tcpdump: permission denied' on first run
mitigation: Before Stage 5 entry (or as SP-001 first-day deliverable): create build_environment.yaml at project root specifying tcpdump privilege strategy (CAP_NET_RAW via setcap, Docker --cap-add NET_RAW, or CI runner privilege level). Reference from SP-001 entry_criteria.

claim: SP-008 entry criterion sprint_status_in_sprint_ledger_for_SP_000_through_SP_007_all_eq_completed_or_qa_gated references a 'sprint_ledger' artifact that does not exist in .product/. Cross-sprint state-of-truth mechanism is implicit (pm_review_approved_sprint_outcome appears in every exit criterion but no canonical artifact is defined for where ProductBuild writes/reads sprint completion).
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Council DevEx perspective; explicit absence of sprint-ledger.yaml or sprint-state.yaml in .product/
mitigation: Stage 5 build-environment setup: define sprint-ledger.jsonl schema (sprint_id, status, completed_at, exits_passed, pm_review_decision_id) and write path; ProductBuild appends on each sprint close, reads on next sprint entry. Defaults to leveraging pm-review-history.jsonl + execution-journal.jsonl if no separate ledger created.

claim: NFR-002 child_process egress block (SP-001 exit) names os_firewall as the enforcement path, but TR-001/TR-002 install requirements (SP-007) do not include a firewall-rule provisioning step. On a Mac without pre-configured pf rule, SP-001 exit criterion passes the JS-land hook test but leaves an unblocked child_process / native-addon path that NFR-002 promises to eliminate. David persona's privileged-documents threat model is incomplete.
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Persona Adversary (David) — only blocking persona objection; cites NFR-002 + TR-001 + ADR-001 directly
mitigation: Two paths: (a) update ADR-001 + SP-001 exit criterion to specify application-level enforcement for child_process via spawn-policy hook (no child_process spawning in v1 pipeline code path; CI lint forbids child_process imports outside whitelist); OR (b) add TR-001 install step that provisions OS firewall rule (pf on macOS, iptables on Linux) and SP-007 exit criterion verifies firewall_rule_active.

claim: SP-008 Maya Week-1 engagement-proxy (≥5 corpus.find queries + ≥1 acceptance event in 7 days) is the very last exit criterion of the very last sprint (after ~45-50 elapsed build days). If the proxy fails, rollback is to Stage 4 recycle (binary cliff). No mid-build engagement-signal check exists.
evidence: Sonnet-4-6 Council PM perspective + Persona Adversary Maya (advisory)
mitigation: Add informal SP-005.5 dogfood checkpoint after SP-005 (corpus.find available) and before SP-006 (storage layer locks): Shon runs ≥10 real corpus.find queries against actual documents, results journaled. Counts toward SP-008 7-day window if instrumented through NFR-016 telemetry. De-risks SP-008 cliff without adding a sprint.

claim: Stage 2 ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature uses per-Feature '# Covers requirements:' comments; handoff-builder.ts pre-fix only recognized per-scenario '# requirement:' tags or inline FR ids in Scenario titles. Build-Ready gate failed with 29 false-negative 'must-haves-have-gherkin' violations on first dry-run
evidence: handoff-builder.ts validate exit_code=1 with 29 failures before fix; exit_code=0 with all 5 guarantees passing after fix
mitigation: Extended gherkinScenariosFor() to walk Feature blocks and count scenarios under any Feature whose '# Covers requirements:' comment lists the FR id (D-017). The .feature file convention remains the source of truth; validator now reads what the file actually declares.

claim: C-018/C-019/C-020 remain status=open in concerns.jsonl despite their mitigation work (three @adversary Gherkin scenarios for F-5/F-8/F-10) being completed and present in ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature as of C-033 closure; the RISK-REGISTER auto-deriving from concerns.jsonl will import these as 3 unresolved high-severity risks
evidence: concerns.jsonl: C-018/019/020 status=open mitigation='Carry-forward: add scenario in Validate stage'; ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature lines 1383-1416 show @adversary @f-5/@f-8/@f-10 scenarios fully authored and tagged; C-033 (stage=4-plan) documents their addition
mitigation: Stage 5 appends status_update entries flipping C-018/019/020 from open to mitigated, citing ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature scenarios at the 'Stage-3 carry-forward adversary tests' Feature; RISK-REGISTER re-derivation will see them as mitigated; TEST-PLAN already wires them via TC-021/035/025

claim: ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature NFR-008 scenario has a vacuous Then clause: 'meets the absolute floor set at Plan stage' — the floor is deferred to a future artifact and not bound to a concrete value or D-NNN reference at scenario eval time
evidence: ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature NFR-008 @should @cf-3 scenario; REQUIREMENTS.yaml NFR-008 test_method also defers; Q-001 in questions.jsonl remains open on the exact value; D-012 / ADR-005 set provisional floor N=20
mitigation: NFR-008 is priority=should and out of must-have TEST-PLAN scope, so does NOT block Build-Ready. SP-000 pre-build pilot per ADR-005 binds floor to a concrete integer (provisionally N=20 per D-012). Post-pilot, Stage 2 owner can amend the scenario to reference D-012 or hard-code the resolved floor. Tracked here for ProductBuild visibility.

Adversary scenario authored in ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature per C-033 (Stage 4) and wired into TEST-PLAN.md (Stage 5). Reference: ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature 'Stage-3 carry-forward adversary tests' / 'binary file with allowlisted extension rejected at MIME-sniff (FR-010 mime_mismatch)' (TC-021). Status updated by RedTeam preflight finding C-039.
claim: status update — see prior C-018 entries for original claim; mitigation now complete

mitigation: Adversary scenario authored in ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature per C-033 (Stage 4) and wired into TEST-PLAN.md (Stage 5). Status updated by RedTeam preflight finding C-039.
claim: status update — see prior C-019 entries for original claim; mitigation now complete

mitigation: Adversary scenario authored in ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature per C-033 (Stage 4) and wired into TEST-PLAN.md (Stage 5). Reference: ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature 'Stage-3 carry-forward adversary tests' / '60MB files identical in first 1MB but differing in tail are ingested as separate documents' (TC-025). Status updated by RedTeam preflight finding C-039.
claim: status update — see prior C-020 entries for original claim; mitigation now complete

mitigation: Closed by D-016 (Stage 4 PM-Review accepted ADR-001..ADR-008, all 8 ADRs status: proposed -> accepted at 2026-04-26T00:35:00Z). Verified: ls ADRs/ shows 8 files, all frontmatter status: accepted.
claim: status update — D-016 explicitly closes C-032

mitigation: Closed by D-016 path (b): TR-001 install side-effect provisions OS-level firewall rule (pf macOS / iptables Linux); SP-007 exit criterion verifies rule active. ADR-001 Decision section updated inline in Stage 4. TC-049 + TC-050 wire the test plan.
claim: status update — D-016 explicitly closes C-036 via path (b)

Stage 5 4-perspective Council (sonnet, cross-model from opus generators) voted composite conditional_go on per-stage gate. 2 blocking objections cite SP-001 preconditions (C-034 build_environment.yaml MUST exist; C-036 firewall path MUST be chosen) — both already tracked. 2 logged-only cite C-037 (SP-005.5 formalization) + C-035 (sprint-ledger.jsonl schema).
evidence: Council output 2026-04-26: RELIABILITY conditional_go cites C-034 + SP-001; SECURITY conditional_go cites C-036 + D-013 (note: C-036 was just flipped to mitigated by current Stage 5 status_update referencing D-016 path (b) — Council did not see this); UX conditional_go cites C-037 + SP-008 + UR-001; BUSINESS conditional_go cites C-035 + SP-008.
mitigation: Per-stage gate.yaml on_conditional_go: conditions append to concerns.jsonl (this entry). C-034 + C-035 + C-037 already tracked with mitigations. C-036 closure (D-016 path (b)) was post-Council; surface to PM-Review for confirmation. C-034 closure is a SP-001 entry-criterion not a Stage 5 build-ready blocker. PM-Review at terminal gate decides go/recycle/kill/hold (conditional_go forbidden per v3 §9 line 276).

Reframed by D-018: build_environment.yaml is a ProductBuild-owned execution-environment contract, not a ProductDevelopment artifact. Carries forward to ProductBuild for shaping; ProductBuild defines its own tcpdump CAP_NET_RAW recipe (setcap path, Docker --cap-add NET_RAW path, or CI runner strategy) when it implements SP-001. ProductDevelopment delivers requirements + tests + roadmap, not infrastructure recipes.
claim: status update — D-018 reframes C-034 as ProductBuild-owned

mitigation: Reframed by D-018: sprint-ledger.jsonl is a ProductBuild-owned progress-recording contract, not a ProductDevelopment artifact. ProductBuild may implement sprint completion as sprint-ledger.jsonl OR reuse pm-review-history.jsonl + execution-journal.jsonl OR define its own schema. ProductDevelopment delivers SPRINT-PLAN.yaml entry/exit criteria; ProductBuild owns the progress-journaling format.
claim: status update — D-018 reframes C-035 as ProductBuild-owned

handoff-builder.ts emits HANDOFF-TO-BUILD.yaml as pure YAML (no markdown-frontmatter '---' delimiters); frontmatter.ts validate expects markdown-with-yaml-frontmatter format and fails with 'no frontmatter found'. The file itself satisfies all 5 v3 §18:413-419 contract guarantees (handoff-builder validate exits 0); the validator drift does not affect the actual contract.
evidence: Stage 5 Step 12 post-write check: frontmatter.ts validate exit=1 with 'no frontmatter found'; handoff-builder.ts validate exit=0 with 'all 5 contract guarantees pass'; the actual file contains schema_version, handoff_generated, from_skill, to_skill, project, artifacts, build_scope, inviolable_decisions, validation, write_back_contracts at top level — exactly per v3 §18 spec.
mitigation: Tool-side drift between two ProductDevelopment validators. Not a Build-Ready blocker (contract guarantees are what v3 §18 specifies; markdown wrapping isn't). Future surgical fix: either (a) update handoff-builder.ts to wrap output in '---' delimiters with a one-line synthetic heading, or (b) update frontmatter.ts validate to accept pure YAML files with .yaml extension as 'frontmatter is the whole document'. Logged for ProductBuild visibility — ProductBuild consumes the file as YAML, which is contractually correct.

claim: corpus.find returns valid hits but `signals_used` is `[]`, violating FR-RETRIEVAL-002 which mandates `['bm25','dense','graph','confidence']` (or the subset that contributed). Observed even when hits ARE returned. Likely cause: SP-006 tier orchestrator wraps the SP-005 hybrid retriever but does not propagate signals_used from the inner SearchOutput to the outer envelope after tier fallthrough.
evidence: Multiple verified queries on pai-node01 2026-05-15 returning {hits: [SearchHit], signals_used: [], tier_used: 'hybrid'}. Direct SQL `documents_fts MATCH 'corpus'` returns a row with a BM25 score, confirming BM25 contributed.
mitigation: SP-007 scope candidate, or polish PR. Fix surface: packages/index/src/tier-orchestrator.ts — propagate the inner SearchOutput.signals_used through when Tier 0 returns hits without fallthrough; aggregate signals across tiers when fallthrough occurs.

claim: packages/storage/src/catalog-md-generator.ts `regenerateCatalogFromDb` SELECTs a `summary` column from the `documents` table. No such column exists — summary lives in body-file frontmatter (SP-004 mirror), not in SQL. Function fails non-fatally on every `corpus reindex` run with `no such column: summary`.
evidence: 2026-05-15 reindex output on pai-node01: `[1/1] doc-9736040c ... embedded + indexed in 604ms; CATALOG.md regeneration failed: no such column: summary`.
mitigation: Either (a) read summary from body-file frontmatter via the SP-002 parseMarkdownWithFrontmatter codec, or (b) elide the summary field from CATALOG.md lines if it's not retrievable, or (c) add a `summary TEXT` column to documents schema (schema migration). Decision belongs in SP-007 scope or a hardening PR. Bug surfaced 2026-05-15.

claim: Cold-start vocabulary UX gap: on a fresh install the established taxonomy is empty, so the SP-004 grammar-constrained classifier rejects every first-time term (domain, type, tag, source_type) it proposes. Documents stall at facet_type='unclassified' indefinitely. No CLI surface exists to promote proposed terms to established. The substrate as installed cannot classify ANY document until vocabulary is seeded via direct SQL.
evidence: 2026-05-15 smoke test on pai-node01: smoke-test doc dropped in inbox; daemon emitted classify.vocabulary_violation on first term (`system-verification` domain). Operator workaround required: INSERT 22 starter domain+type rows directly via sqlite3 CLI, then 28 tag+source_type rows. The user has no documented path between drop-file and first-successful-classify.
mitigation: Three paths viable: (1) implement `corpus taxonomy promote --proposed <term>` CLI in SP-007; (2) add an auto-promote-first-occurrence policy (changes the contract; needs ADR); (3) ship a 90-second first-run with a curated 50-term seed (NFR-014 / TR-002 candidate). Recommend (1) + (3) together for SP-007 to honor the 'install + 90s first run' goal.

claim: SP-006 review processes (feature-dev:code-reviewer + QATester + /simplify) did not catch the MCP transport cutover gap because the acceptance criteria for 'transport cutover' tasks ended at the library-handler level. No end-to-end test exercises CLI → MCP stdio → tool-call → ranked hits path against a real index. Engineer #5's cutover task was marked done because createCorpusFindHandler called runTieredSearch — but the CLI never wired createCorpusFindHandler into the running server.
evidence: SP-006 had 187 test files / 878 passing tests, CI green, Constitution 16/16, three review agents passed. The bug manifested on first production-install smoke test against the running MCP server. Root cause: testing the change in isolation passes; testing the deployed surface end-to-end was not in scope.
mitigation: Update ProductDevelopment skill acceptance-criteria templates: any task that includes 'cutover X to Y' or 'wire X into production path' MUST have an end-to-end scenario that spawns the production binary, exercises the real RPC, asserts the result. Reviewer agent rubrics need a 'is the changed surface reachable from production entry point?' check. Surface as a skill-template improvement, not a code change in this repo.

claim: Pallas-side process drift: between SP-006 merge (2026-05-14) and this retro (2026-05-15), Pallas shipped 2 code-change commits to main (6b8ba22 classifier model swap, 53c7bc2 MCP wire-up + keep_alive) without spec-kit feature, ADR amendment, ledger decision, sprint-plan reference, or new test coverage for keep_alive behavior. Violates feedback-sprint-process-only and feedback-pallas-drives-tactical-workflow.
evidence: git log 5237916..HEAD shows 5 commits to main since SP-006 merge: 3 docs (acceptable), 2 code fixes (violate sprint discipline). No specs/00N entry created for the fixes; no decisions logged until this retro.
mitigation: This retro + ledger backfill (D-023..D-027, C-043..C-047) restores the audit trail. Going forward: bug fixes that touch production code paths route through either (a) a new spec-kit feature under specs/ if the change is substantive, or (b) a documented hotfix-with-retro entry that pairs the commit with an immediate ledger D-NNN. Discipline noted in user direction 2026-05-15 17:00 PT.

claim: SP-007 telemetry event classes use severity: 'info'|'warning'|'error' + outcome: 'success'|'failure' enums per data-model.md verbatim; the SP-001..SP-006 existing TelemetryEvent union uses severity: 'warn' + outcome: 'failed' spellings. The two vocabularies coexist in the Zod discriminated union (open by construction) but produce a slight shape difference between SP-007 events and prior-sprint events in telemetry.jsonl.
evidence: packages/contracts/src/telemetry.ts SP-007 variants (install.*, uninstall.*, taxonomy.*) use the data-model.md vocabulary; prior-sprint variants use the 'warn'|'failed' vocabulary. Documented in tasks.md T013 NOTE during Phase 2 Engineer #1 implementation.
mitigation: Acceptable for now — the Zod discriminated union is open per variant. Future polish PR could normalize to a single severity / outcome vocabulary across all telemetry classes (mechanical refactor; touches ~12 SP-007 variants OR all SP-001..SP-006 variants depending on which direction is normative).

claim: Phase 9 live perf-measurement on pai-node01 (T088-T091) deferred to operator first install. The 90-second install ceiling + 30-second smoke ceiling are AbortController-enforced, NOT measured-median commitments. The quickstart.md 'Honest performance notes' section documents the deferral honestly per Constitution Principle XVI.
evidence: specs/007-install-first-run/quickstart.md 'Honest performance notes' section + the AbortController ceilings in packages/pipeline/src/policies.ts (installPolicy.installBudgetMs = 90000, smokeBudgetMs = 30000).
mitigation: Operator's first install on a clean machine produces the canonical p95. Update the quickstart.md performance table once measured. Not a deliverable blocker; the contract is the AbortController ceiling, not a median commitment.

claim: R3 operator-friction risk: the manual corpus accept <request-id> workflow per Decision D is intentional v1 friction. Operator must read the telemetry NDJSON stream, copy a UUID, and run a separate CLI command for every load-bearing result. If Track B PASS lands but the operator reports the workflow as unusable (placebo-accept on every result OR never-accept despite useful results), the metric is corrupted in both directions.
evidence: specs/008-user-acceptance/quickstart.md Step 4 + R3 risk-register entry in plan.md. Decision D rejected --last flag for v1.
mitigation: Track B verdict will surface the operator's real friction. If PASS verdict lands AND operator reports the manual flow as workable, ship v1.0.0 as-is. If PASS verdict lands but operator complains, ship v1.0.0 (the metric is valid) and queue v1.1.0 polish work for --last flag or session-end rate prompt. If Track B verdict is FAIL non-KILL specifically because operator never pressed accept (despite useful results), the metric is the symptom and the v1 friction is the cause — add the UX polish before re-running dogfood.

claim: R5 daemon-down-during-dogfood-window risk: if the operator's daemon crashes (kill-9, systemd restart, machine reboot, OS update) during the 7-day Track B window, the engagement telemetry has a gap. The C-046 E2E smoke confirms the report tolerates malformed/missing rows (parse_errors_count + continues), but a multi-day gap could push queries-in-window below the C-028 threshold artificially, producing a false FAIL.
evidence: FR-ENGAGEMENT-003 + SC-008-021 ('Malformed lines skipped + counted; report still emits'). The report is robust to skipped lines but not to wall-clock gaps.
mitigation: Operator runs `systemctl --user status corpus.service` (Linux) or `launchctl print user/$UID/com.llm-corpus.daemon` (macOS) at least once per day during the Track B window. If a multi-day gap is detected at end-of-window, the operator extends the window past the gap and re-runs the report. A future polish PR could add a `corpus engagement-proxy report --include-uptime` flag that detects gaps via missing telemetry heartbeats and emits a soft-warning.

claim: Track B verdict PENDING at PR-merge: SC-008-035 (the Maya Week-1 engagement-proxy verdict) cannot be satisfied at sprint close per Constitution Principle XVI. The 7-day operator dogfood window is the SOLE source of the verdict; the PR-merge ships Track A only. This is not a defect but is recorded explicitly so the post-merge tracking is unambiguous.
evidence: specs/008-user-acceptance/RETROSPECTIVE.md Track B Verdict block (RESERVED until end-of-window).
mitigation: Operator runs the workflow in specs/008-user-acceptance/quickstart.md; at end-of-window pastes the corpus engagement-proxy report --since=$DOGFOOD_START output (text + JSON) into the Track B Verdict block; opens a follow-up PR titled 'SP-008 Track B verdict: <PASS|FAIL|KILL>'. v1.0.0 release ceremony triggers on PASS.
